responses: Hello 👋 I am your potato crop assistant. How can I help you today?
Hi 👋 I can help you detect diseases and guide you on treatment.
Good morning 🌤 Tell me what’s happening with your crops.
Good evening 🌙 Ask me anything about your plants.
Nice to meet you {name} 👨‍🌾 I will help you take care of your crops.
Got it 👍 I will remember your name, Farmer {name}.
Welcome back {name} 👋 Let’s check your crops.

late blight.name: Late Blight
late blight.cause: A fungus-like organism spreads quickly in cool, wet conditions.
late blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots on leaves, stems, and tubers with a white fuzzy edge.
late blight.treatment: Remove infected plants, improve drainage, and use a safe fungicide if needed.
late blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid overhead watering, and rotate crops yearly.
late blight.advice: Late blight spreads fast. Remove infected leaves, keep plants dry, and protect the crop with a fungicide if needed.

early blight.name: Early Blight
early blight.cause: A fungus grows on older leaves when the weather is warm and humid.
early blight.symptoms: Small brown spots with rings that grow into larger leaf patches.
early blight.treatment: Remove the damaged leaves, improve airflow, and apply a protective spray if it keeps spreading.
early blight.prevention: Space plants well, keep leaves dry, and rotate crops to reduce disease carryover.
early blight.advice: Early blight can be managed by removing bad leaves, keeping plants airy, and spraying only if needed.

bacterial wilt.name: Bacterial Wilt
bacterial wilt.cause: Soil bacteria enter the plant through wounds or insect damage.
bacterial wilt.symptoms: Plants wilt quickly, especially on hot days, and leaves may turn yellow before collapsing.
bacterial wilt.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plants. Do not compost them near healthy crops.
bacterial wilt.prevention: Use clean seed potatoes, avoid injury to plants, and rotate crops away from potatoes.
bacterial wilt.advice: Bacterial wilt is serious. Remove sick plants and stop the spread by using clean seed potato and gentle handling.

healthy.name: Healthy
healthy.cause: Good care and balanced growing conditions keep plants strong.
healthy.symptoms: Green leaves, sturdy stems, and no obvious disease spots or damage.
healthy.treatment: Keep watering evenly, use compost or balanced fertilizer, and inspect plants regularly.
healthy.prevention: Keep soil healthy, rotate crops, and remove weeds or debris near plants.
healthy.advice: Your crop looks healthy. Continue even watering, good soil care, and regular checks so it stays that way.

spraying.advice: Spray in the early morning when leaves are dry. Use the right product and follow instructions closely.

watering.advice: Water evenly and avoid waterlogging. Check the soil before you water, and keep roots moist but not soggy.

removal.advice: Remove badly infected leaves or plants and destroy them away from the field. This stops disease from spreading.

prevention_general.advice: Keep plants healthy with good soil, proper spacing, clean tools, and crop rotation each year.
